page: Amazon Web Services m4.10xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server built on the Xen hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 40 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 20 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 2.4 GHz. It is equipped with 160.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and standard CPU processing. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile characterized by moderate multi-core capabilities and weaker single-core execution. Single-core metrics, including stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, fall into the bottom 25% tier, reflecting the older generation architecture of the Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor. Conversely, multi-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core, consistently rank in the middle 50% tier, leveraging the high core count of 40 vCPUs. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes also demonstrate average performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m4.10xlarge offers a balanced resource density of compute and memory without the premium costs associated with newer generation architectures or specialized hardware like local SSDs and GPUs. The trade-off is lower single-threaded efficiency and slower instruction execution for modern workloads. This server is best suited for general-purpose workloads that benefit from parallel processing and moderate memory capacity, such as static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and Redis caching.
description: Amazon Web Services m4.10xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server running on the Xen hypervisor. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor with x86_64 architecture, it delivers 40 dedicated vCPUs across 20 physical cores and 160.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, but features a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct trade-off: single-core performance is weak, ranking in the bottom 25%, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average middle 50% range. This makes the server qualitatively cost-effective for parallelized, resource-balanced tasks rather than single-threaded speed. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, multi-threaded data processing, database operations, and Redis caching.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m4.10xlarge is a general-purpose server with 40 vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and a 10 Gbps network baseline, offering balanced multi-core performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m4.10xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 40 vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance for standard workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server offering high core density and stable multi-threaded performance for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Features 40 dedicated vCPUs and 160.0 GB of system memory
Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor on x86_64 architecture
Delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Suited for static web serving, database operations, and Redis caching
